text: $BTC - Bad news, decent response. Interesting spot, think you can make a good bull case here. Weekly support, negative macro news entirely disregarded. Maybe time for some up for at least a little bit, we have room up to $80k without flipping bullish.

$BTC update: Sluggishly moving through support. If it gives there isn't much in terms of support below it. I think the stock market has to implode for us to go to the level below it, if not we'll probably just get chop. In any case, nothing for me to do just yet.

$BTC update: Another disappointing close. I think even if you're an aggressive bull you'd wanna see a close above at least $71k. Otherwise you're just buying slow bleed. I'm not in a rush so I'm waiting for a deeper discount ($42k) or a market structure shift (above $86k).

Unfortunate price action. I saw people celebrate the 6% move a couple days ago. Now everyone is back to being depressed. I legit think we need the US to return to become a somewhat sane country again before we can have any meaningful bounce. If they don't, it's just chop or down.
$ETH HTF Range reclaim. Only resistance left is $3800 if that breaks I'll expect new ATHs. Invalidation for bulls is a close sub $2200. Pretty good chart IMO.
notes: ETH TA, price targets, invalidation levels

text: $SOL people making fun of $ETH's performance is really funny in hindsight. SOL seems to be chasing ETH. I made this post more than a year ago, still rings true.

The second scheduled 'Blob Parameter Only (BPO)' fork is now live on Ethereum mainnet! This has increased the Ethereum network's blob target from 10 to 14 and increased the blob limit from 15 to 21. This upgrade allows for even greater scalability for layer 2s!
notes: Protocol upgrade, blob parameters, L2 scalability

text: Ethereum Pectra and Fusaka upgrades live last year. The next big upgrade is Glamsterdam which introduces ePBS and BALs as major features with a long list of other EIPs.
After being an avid supporter of FOCIL (and earlier iterations) for years, I'm extremely happy to see that it's going to go live in Hegota. Huge win for the ecosystem!

Quantum is a very real threat for blockchains and it's coming sooner than most people think. The EF's new 'Post Quantum' team will ensure that Ethereum is able to defend itself against any and all quantum threats.

There is a very possible timeline where Ethereum becomes quantum resistant before the centralised financial system does. Ethereum will be the global quantum-resistant settlement layer for all types of value.
agentic-coded an Ethereum client targeting the 2030+ roadmap. 702K lines of Go. 65 roadmap items. Syncs with mainnet.
notes: Ethereum client implementation, roadmap — infrastructure not agents

text: FOCIL (EIP-7805) was SFI'd and is the CL headliner for the Hegota fork. Ethereum has decided to prioritize a feature that improves censorship resistance, gives better inclusion guarantees to its users, and strengthens its position as the most credibly neutral network.
notes: EIP technical detail, CL fork feature

text: Blockspace is abundant. Decentralized, permissionless and resilient blockspace is not. Ethereum must first and foremost be decentralized, permissionless and resilient blockspace - and then make that abundant.
thedailygwei: FOCIL in Hegota, Core dev 2026 priorities, Major Base update.
100ms block times are now live on Abstract testnet. Mainnet soon.
Testing out 100ms block times on Abstract right now. Let's go even faster.

The biggest factor holding back rapid iteration and innovation in crypto has been audits. This brings us closer to a world where developers can quickly ship safe smart contracts and build at the same speed as traditional software development.
notes: Smart contract audit tooling, developer infrastructure

text: We're doubling down on onchain builders at Abstract. If you're tinkering onchain, we want to help you with GTM, engineering, and product design. There has never been a better time in history to build onchain products.

In order to push the boundaries of what can be done onchain, we need to prioritize the apps that natively leverage smart contracts as building blocks for their products. It's as important as ever during a bear market to keep the spirit of onchain innovation alive.

text: Introducing the Abstract MCP Server. Agents can now manage Abstract global wallets, read Portal data, and interact with the chain. This server works with any MCP-compatible client including OpenClaw and Claude.

text: Why crypto will be the monetary system for AI agents: permissionless access, programmability, and no middleman. No KYC, no spending limits, infinite wallets with controlled limits. For merchants: no chargeback risk, stablecoins, guaranteed transparent transactions. Micropayments for even cheaper access.
notes: Crypto as AI agent payment layer — agent economics

text: Current workflow for agents: human acquires API key, human pays via credit card, human puts key in agent settings. Workflow with x402: human sets up crypto wallet, agent pays for all API usage by itself. This is the future.
Everyone is launching their own OpenClaw competitor. First Notion, then Anthropic, now Perplexity. There's never been a better time to build skills and MCP servers for agents.

text: Introducing @ClankerZone_. The social trading platform for @openclaw agents. An onchain arena where agents interact with each other through launching, trading, and posting thoughts on different tokens. Powered by @privy_io, @clanker_world, and ERC-8004.
